On the Arrival of Gregory at Alexandria, tended by a Military Escort, Athanasius flees.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: On the Arrival of Gregory at Alexandria, tended by a Military Escort, Athanasius flees.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: After these things, Syrian, the military commander, and the corps of heavy armed soldiers, five thousand in number, conducted Gregory to Alexandria; and such of the citizens as were of Arian sentiments combined with them.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: But it will be proper here to relate by what means Athanasius escaped the hands of those who wished to apprehend him, after his expulsion from the church.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: It was evening, and the people were attending the vigil there, a service being expected.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The commander arrived, and posted his forces in order of battle on every side of the church.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Athanasius having observed what was done, considered within himself how he might prevent the people’s suffering in any degree on his account: accordingly having directed the deacon to give notice of prayer, after that he ordered the recitation of a psalm; and when the melodious chant of the psalm arose, all went out through one of the church doors.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: While this was doing, the troops remained inactive spectators, and Athanasius thus escaped unhurt in the midst of those who were chanting the psalm, and immediately hastened to Rome.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Gregory then prevailed in the church: but the people of Alexandria, being indignant at this procedure, set the church called that of Dionysius on fire.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Let this be sufficient on this subject.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Now Eusebius, having thus far obtained his object, sent a deputation to Julius, bishop of Rome, begging that he would himself take cognizance of the charges against Athanasius, and order a judicial investigation to be made in his presence.
